matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: An observed PRKAA1 variant places this sample in the curated PRKAA1 research context. The local PRKAA1 knowledge base is built around metabolic and cancer-association literature. Common PRKAA1 variants are best interpreted as pathway modifiers rather than as highly penetrant clinical alleles. Common PRKAA1 variants are typically interpreted as low-penetrance metabolic or cancer-risk modifiers.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: PRKAA1 is central to AMPK signaling, nutrient sensing, mitochondrial stress responses, and mTOR restraint.
The strongest common-variant literature centers on gastric-cancer susceptibility and metabolic-endocrine phenotypes.
Energy-stress and nutrient-sensing biology.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a PRKAA1 variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. PRKAA1 methylation should be read as energy-sensing pathway context. Promoter-proximal methylation may suggest altered catalytic-subunit expression, but interpretation remains broader than any one common SNP. The local PRKAA1 knowledge base is built around metabolic and cancer-association literature. Common PRKAA1 variants are best interpreted as pathway modifiers rather than as highly penetrant clinical alleles. Common PRKAA1 variants are typically interpreted as low-penetrance metabolic or cancer-risk modifiers.
